candidates.direction_name: Retime the PTX barrier seam on the current correctness-safe 128x128 anchor

score_breakdown.reason: Best current-anchor exploit after the writer-shape family flattened out.

ranking_notes: This is now the cleanest local seam on the current correctness-safe anchor.
It does not depend on preserving the round-7 low-register writer signal.
candidates.hypothesis: The last two writer-family iterations both landed back on the same 198-register / 16.6%-active-warps regime, so the cheapest remaining local seam on the correct anchor is the cp.async wait-group plus CTA barrier handoff. A bounded retime can target the persistent 5.95% barrier stall and 6.85% long-scoreboard stall without touching writer coverage or geometry.
candidates.expected_bottleneck: synchronization_barrier_issue layered on top of occupancy_latency_hiding_issue in the current correctness-safe 128x128 PTX anchor
candidates.stop_condition: Stop if barrier retiming breaks correctness, grows shared memory, or improves barrier without any runtime win.

target_hotspots.explanation: Barrier remains the most actionable local stall seam on the current correct anchor.
Any handoff retime must not simply trade barrier time for worse scoreboard pressure.

expected_local_changes: Retune cp.async wait-group completion versus CTA-wide sync on the steady-state PTX path.
Keep writer coverage and the 22016-byte shared-memory budget fixed.
Do not mix barrier work with another writer sweep rewrite.

guardrail_metrics.reason: This stays on the current correctness-safe anchor.
Do not improve barrier by worsening scoreboard stalls.

candidates.direction_name: Apply only a minimal PTX export-address cleanup on the correct anchor

score_breakdown.reason: Smaller and safer than the recent writer rewrites, but still secondary to the barrier seam.

ranking_notes: This is intentionally narrower than the recent writer-shape attempts.
It stays below dir_01 because the last two rounds showed that writer-side work can easily expand into bigger changes than intended.
Keep row coverage and helper structure fixed.
candidates.hypothesis: The broader writer rewrites did not hold, but there can still be a small low-risk scalar cleanup left inside the correct PTX writer path. Restrict the change to late pointer conversion and row/column-derived scalar lifetimes only, without changing row coverage or helper topology.
candidates.expected_bottleneck: occupancy_latency_hiding_issue with a small tail_overhead_or_generic_path_issue in the PTX export address math
candidates.stop_condition: Stop if the cleanup touches row coverage again or cannot stay correctness-safe.

target_hotspots.explanation: A true minimal cleanup should not make the current register floor worse.

expected_local_changes: Push pointer conversion as late as possible in the correct PTX writer.
Do not change the row sweep or helper topology.
Use this only as a scalar-lifetime cleanup.

guardrail_metrics.reason: This must remain a correctness-safe cleanup.

candidates.direction_name: Transplant the lower-register half-panel budget into the correctness-safe 256x128 pivot

score_breakdown.reason: Best orthogonal branch once the local PTX exploit sequence is exhausted.

ranking_notes: Keep this alive so the queue does not collapse to only PTX-local work.
It stays third because the current PTX anchor still has one cleaner local seam left.
Use it when the barrier family also flattens out.
candidates.hypothesis: If the local PTX anchor stays stuck at 198 registers and 16.6% active warps after the barrier exploit, the next meaningful move is the correctness-safe 256x128 pivot with the lower-register half-panel budget transplanted into it. That remains the best orthogonal branch against the same occupancy ceiling.
candidates.expected_bottleneck: occupancy_latency_hiding_issue attacked through geometry and register-budget change rather than another PTX-local cleanup
candidates.stop_condition: Stop if the pivot transplant broadens beyond the hot-band path, fails correctness, or does not improve the occupancy signature.

target_hotspots.explanation: The point of the pivot is to move off the current register floor entirely.

expected_local_changes: Transplant only the lower-register half-panel staging and ownership choices.
Keep the change limited to the hot-band branch and launch selection.

guardrail_metrics.reason: This branch has a wider correctness surface and must stay clean.

notes: Projected exactly three node_b directions into normalized search candidates.
